nodes.name: HomomorphicFilter
nodes.description: Applies a homomorphic filter to a signal by operating in the log-frequency domain: takes the logarithm of the signal magnitude, applies a frequency-domain filter (typically a high-pass or emphasis filter) via FFT, then exponentiates back to recover a filtered signal with compressed dynamic range and enhanced high-frequency components.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: non-zero values required before log transform; length >= 1

outputs.name: filtered_signal
outputs.type_desc: 1-D numeric array (time-domain filtered signal, same length as input)

nodes.type_signature: (signal: 1-D numeric array (time-domain signal samples), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D numeric array (time-domain filtered signal, same length as input)
nodes.conceptual_summary: LogDomain_FrequencyEmphasisFilter. Transforms a 1-D positive-valued scalar sequence into a logarithmic representation, applies a spectral shaping kernel (e.g., high-pass emphasis) via forward and inverse Fourier transforms, then inverts the logarithmic mapping through exponentiation to recover a signal in the original multiplicative domain. This composition converts multiplicative interference into additive structure, enabling linear filtering to separate components that are entangled multiplicatively in the original space. The result preserves the signal's length while compressing dynamic range and selectively amplifying high-frequency structural content.. Applications: Geophysical seismic deconvolution: separating a subsurface reflectivity sequence (fast-varying, high-frequency) from a slowly varying source wavelet that is multiplicatively convolved into the recorded trace — the log-domain transform linearizes the convolution so a high-pass filter cleanly isolates the reflectivity component, Computational imaging / illumination normalization: removing spatially smooth, low-frequency illumination gradients (modeled as a multiplicative field) from a 2-D intensity array to reveal underlying high-frequency texture detail — the same log-then-filter-then-exponentiate pipeline applies column- or row-wise across the raster, Biomedical time-series preprocessing: decomposing a measured physiological waveform (e.g., respiratory or cardiac signal) in which a slow-varying envelope modulates a faster oscillatory carrier; log-domain high-pass filtering isolates the rapid-fluctuation component from the low-frequency trend without needing an explicit envelope estimator, Materials characterization / spectroscopy: suppressing broad-band baseline drift in a spectral intensity curve (Raman, FTIR, NMR) that multiplicatively scales sharp resonance peaks; log transformation converts the baseline to an additive low-frequency component that is trivially removed by spectral high-pass emphasis, recovering normalized peak structure

nodes.name: validate_and_normalize_inputs
nodes.description: Validates that the signal is provided and casts sampling_rate to float, raising a TypeError if signal is None.
nodes.type_signature: (signal: 1-D numeric array or None, sampling_rate: int or float) -> tuple[1-D numeric array, float]

nodes.name: design_lowpass_filter
nodes.description: Designs a 1st-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 8 Hz cutoff using the given sampling rate, returning second-order sections (SOS) coefficients for zero-phase filtering.

nodes.name: compute_log_magnitude
nodes.description: Transforms the time-domain signal into the log-magnitude domain by taking the element-wise absolute value followed by the natural logarithm, preparing it for linear (cepstral) filtering.

nodes.name: apply_lowpass_filter_in_log_domain
nodes.description: Applies the designed low-pass SOS filter to the log-magnitude signal using zero-phase forward-backward filtering (sosfiltfilt), effectively retaining the slowly varying envelope component while rejecting high-frequency oscillations in the log domain.

outputs.name: filtered_log_signal
outputs.constraints: same length as input, zero-phase filtered

nodes.type_signature: (sos_coefficients: 2-D numeric array, log_magnitude_signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float array

nodes.name: exponentiate_to_envelope
nodes.description: Maps the filtered log-domain signal back to the amplitude domain by applying the element-wise exponential, recovering the homomorphic (non-normalized) envelope of the original signal.

outputs.name: homomorphic_envelope
outputs.constraints: positive values, same length as input signal
